title: Committed review treats vendored-heavy diffs as limited actionable scope

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark count-only vendored-file reporting and the explicit vendored-heavy scope callout in committed review output.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models a committed-review PR where the actionable surface is limited because most changed files are vendored SDK updates.

description: A committed-review scenario where a pull request is dominated by vendored SDK churn plus a small non-vendored control-surface change. The correct review should report only the skipped vendored-file count, explicitly call out the vendored-heavy scope, and avoid telling contributors to edit vendored files directly.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models a committed-review PR where the actionable surface is limited because most changed files are vendored SDK updates.

mustCatch.description: The review should explicitly call out that the committed diff is vendored-heavy and report only the skipped vendored-file count.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise findings that tell the contributor to modify files under vendor/** directly.

notes: Use this case to benchmark count-only vendored-file reporting and the explicit vendored-heavy scope callout in committed review output.
